enrichment.short_description: Namecheap is a domain registrar and web hosting company offering domain registration, shared/VPS hosting, SSL certificates, email, and a VPN.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Namecheap: domain registration, shared and VPS hosting, SSL certificates, email hosting, and a VPN, with a 30-day free hosting trial.

features.title: Domain registration
features.description: Register domains across 900+ TLDs, with .com starting at $6.79/year for new customers.

features.title: Shared hosting
features.description: Shared hosting plans include free SSL and a 30-day free trial, aimed at startups and personal sites.

features.title: SSL certificates
features.description: Standalone SSL certificates start at $5.99/year, with 50 free Standard SSLs bundled into hosting plans.

features.title: VPN
features.description: Namecheap offers its own consumer VPN service to secure devices and public Wi-Fi browsing.

features.title: PremiumDNS
features.description: A managed DNS service, priced under $5/year, for faster and more reliable domain resolution.

pros: Domain prices are competitive, especially for new customers
Shared hosting includes a 30-day free trial
Bundles SSL certificates with hosting plans
Offers domains, hosting, SSL, DNS, and VPN under one account
cons: Renewal prices for domains and hosting are typically higher than first-year promotional rates
Standalone SSL installation service is a paid add-on ($28.88/year) for non-Namecheap hosting

faqs.question: Does Namecheap offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Shared Hosting plans include a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: How much does a .com domain cost?
faqs.answer: New customers can register a .com domain starting at $6.79 for the first year.

faqs.question: Does hosting include SSL?
faqs.answer: Yes, Shared Hosting plans include 50 free Standard SSL certificates for one year.

faqs.question: Does Namecheap offer a VPN?
faqs.answer: Yes, Namecheap sells its own VPN service for securing devices and public Wi-Fi connections.

use_cases.title: Registering a business domain
use_cases.description: Search, register, and manage domain names across hundreds of TLDs.

use_cases.title: Launching a first website
use_cases.description: Combine shared hosting, a free SSL, and a domain to get a site online quickly.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Namecheap?
paragraphs: Namecheap is a domain registrar and hosting company, founded in 2000 and based in Phoenix, Arizona, that sells domain names across more than 900 extensions alongside shared hosting, SSL certificates, email hosting, PremiumDNS, and a consumer VPN.
The company positions itself around affordable domain pricing and bundles free SSL certificates into its hosting plans.

enrichment.short_description: OVHcloud is a French cloud provider offering VPS, dedicated servers, public and private cloud, web hosting, and domain registration.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OVHcloud offers VPS, dedicated servers, public/private cloud, and web hosting from 46 datacenters, serving 1.6 million customers globally.

features.title: VPS hosting
features.description: VPS plans start from $4.54/month with configurations up to 8 vCores and 24GB RAM.

features.title: Dedicated servers
features.description: Multiple server tiers including Advance (T3, from $124/month), Scale, and High Grade (T5) options.

features.title: Public Cloud
features.description: Compute, storage, networking, managed databases, analytics, and AI/ML services.

features.title: Private Cloud
features.description: VMware, Nutanix, and SAP HANA-based private cloud solutions.

features.title: Web hosting and domains
features.description: Eco, Business, and Agency web hosting plans plus domain registration across 900+ extensions.

features.title: Email hosting
features.description: Microsoft Exchange hosting services for business email.

pros: Transparent pricing with no hidden fees or lock-in charges cited on the site
Large global footprint: 46 datacenters across 4 continents
Broad product range spanning VPS, dedicated, public and private cloud
Data sovereignty controls over storage location and jurisdiction
States a lower environmental footprint (7x less water use, PUE of 1.24) versus competitors
cons: Higher-tier dedicated servers (e.g. Advance T3) start at $124/month, a jump from entry VPS pricing
Headquarters is stated on other OVHcloud pages, not the main site, requiring cross-referencing

faqs.question: What does OVHcloud offer?
faqs.answer: VPS, dedicated servers, public and private cloud, web hosting, domains, and Exchange email hosting.

faqs.question: How many datacenters does OVHcloud operate?
faqs.answer: OVHcloud operates 46 datacenters across 4 continents with 100 Tbit/s of network capacity.

faqs.question: When was OVHcloud founded?
faqs.answer: OVHcloud was founded in 1999 and is headquartered in Roubaix, France.

faqs.question: How much do VPS plans cost?
faqs.answer: VPS plans start from $4.54/month.

use_cases.title: Running a VPS or dedicated server
use_cases.description: Deploy virtual or dedicated infrastructure with predictable, transparent pricing.

use_cases.title: Data sovereignty-sensitive workloads
use_cases.description: Use European infrastructure with control over data storage location and jurisdiction.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OVHcloud?
paragraphs: OVHcloud is a cloud computing and web hosting provider founded in 1999 and headquartered in Roubaix, France, serving 1.6 million customers with infrastructure spanning 46 datacenters across 4 continents.
Its product range covers VPS, dedicated servers, public and private cloud (VMware, Nutanix, SAP HANA), web hosting, domain registration, and Microsoft Exchange email hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Vultr is a cloud infrastructure platform offering on-demand cloud compute, bare metal, and Kubernetes across 32+ global data center regions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vultr offers cloud compute, bare metal, and Kubernetes hosting across 32+ data center regions, with a free plan and instant provisioning.

features.title: Cloud Compute
features.description: On-demand virtual machines, including the newer VX1 line, VX1 available from 2 to 192 vCPUs.

features.title: High Performance Compute
features.description: AMD/Intel CPU and NVMe-backed instances starting at $6/month for dev, test, and small workloads.

features.title: Free tier
features.description: A free plan includes 1 vCPU, 0.5GB RAM, 10GB disk, and 2TB of global bandwidth.

features.title: Bare metal servers
features.description: Dedicated bare metal hardware alongside virtualized compute options.

features.title: Kubernetes
features.description: Managed Kubernetes offered as part of the cloud compute product line.

features.title: Global data centers
features.description: 32 cloud data center regions across six continents.

pros: Offers a genuinely free tier (1 vCPU/0.5GB RAM/10GB disk/2TB bandwidth)
Entry-level paid plans start as low as $6/month
Wide global footprint with 32 data center regions
Instances can provision in under 15 seconds per the VX1 product page
Flat, worldwide egress pricing at $0.01/GB beyond free bandwidth
cons: Newest VX1 generation starts significantly higher ($43.80/month) than legacy compute tiers
Detailed full pricing requires visiting a separate pricing/FAQ page rather than the homepage

faqs.question: Does Vultr have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vultr's free tier includes 1 vCPU, 0.5GB RAM, 10GB disk, and 2TB of bandwidth.

faqs.question: How much does Vultr cost?
faqs.answer: High Performance Compute starts at $6/month; the newer VX1 line starts at $43.80/month.

faqs.question: Where is Vultr headquartered?
faqs.answer: Vultr is headquartered in West Palm Beach, Florida, and was founded in 2014.

faqs.question: Does Vultr offer bare metal servers?
faqs.answer: Yes, alongside virtualized cloud compute and Kubernetes.

use_cases.title: Hosting dev/test environments
use_cases.description: Spin up low-cost or free compute instances for development and small workloads.

use_cases.title: Global low-latency deployment
use_cases.description: Deploy across 32 regions on six continents to reduce latency for end users.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Vultr?
paragraphs: Vultr is a cloud infrastructure platform founded in 2014 and headquartered in West Palm Beach, Florida, offering cloud compute, bare metal servers, and managed Kubernetes across 32 data center regions on six continents.
It offers a free compute tier as well as paid plans starting at $6/month, alongside its newer VX1 compute line for higher-performance workloads.

enrichment.short_description: Linode, part of Akamai, is a cloud computing platform offering compute, Kubernetes, storage, managed databases, and networking with flat pricing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Linode (Akamai Connected Cloud) offers compute, Kubernetes, storage, and managed databases with flat, predictable pricing and low egress fees.

features.title: Compute
features.description: Essential Compute, GPU, Accelerated Compute, Kubernetes, and App Platform offerings.

features.title: Storage
features.description: Block Storage, Object Storage, and Backups.

features.title: Networking
features.description: Cloud Firewall, DNS Manager, NodeBalancers, and private networking.

features.title: Managed Databases
features.description: Managed MySQL and PostgreSQL database services.

features.title: Linode Managed
features.description: An optional managed-services add-on for infrastructure oversight.

pros: Flat, predictable pricing model with low egress fees per the site
Backed by Akamai's global network since its 2022 acquisition
Cloud portability without proprietary lock-in, per Linode's own positioning
Broad product set spanning compute, storage, networking, and databases
cons: Now operates under Akamai ownership rather than as an independent company
Specific instance pricing isn't shown on the main pricing page, which redirects to Akamai's cloud pricing tool without listing figures

faqs.question: Is Linode still independent?
faqs.answer: No, Linode was acquired by Akamai Technologies in 2022 and now operates as Akamai Connected Cloud.

faqs.question: When was Linode founded?
faqs.answer: Linode was founded in 2003 and was originally headquartered in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

faqs.question: What databases does Linode manage?
faqs.answer: Linode offers managed MySQL and PostgreSQL database services.

faqs.question: Does Linode offer Kubernetes?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kubernetes is included among its compute offerings.

use_cases.title: Deploying low-latency applications
use_cases.description: Run edge-native applications across Linode's globally distributed regions.

use_cases.title: Managed database hosting
use_cases.description: Use managed MySQL or PostgreSQL instead of self-hosting a database.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Linode?
paragraphs: Linode, founded in 2003 in Philadelphia and acquired by Akamai Technologies in 2022, is a cloud computing platform now marketed as Akamai Connected Cloud, offering compute, Kubernetes, storage, networking, and managed databases.
The platform emphasizes flat, bundled pricing (CPU, transfer, storage, and RAM combined) and low, predictable egress fees rather than a-la-carte billing.

enrichment.short_description: Hetzner Online is a German hosting provider offering dedicated servers, cloud servers, storage, and web hosting at competitive prices.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hetzner offers affordable dedicated servers, cloud servers, and hosting from Germany, with free firewalls, one-click apps, and load balancing.

features.title: Dedicated servers
features.description: Multiple lines including EX, AX, RX, SX, GPU, and DX (Dell-branded) dedicated server options.

features.title: Cloud servers
features.description: Cost-Optimized, Regular Performance, and General Purpose (dedicated vCPU) cloud server tiers.

features.title: Storage
features.description: Object Storage, Storage Box, and Storage Share offerings.

features.title: Networking add-ons
features.description: Free stateful firewalls, load balancers, and private networking.

features.title: One-click apps
features.description: Free one-click installs for Docker, WordPress, Nextcloud, GitLab, and more.

pros: Long-standing provider founded in 1997 with an established reputation for low prices
Free API/CLI access, firewalls, and one-click apps included
Won Platinum awards in 2026 Service Provider Awards categories ('Superscaler', 'Data Center/Colocation XXL')
Three distinct cloud server tiers to match cost vs. performance needs
cons: Exact server pricing is not shown on the main marketing pages and requires visiting product-specific pages
Headquarters in Germany may mean fewer data center regions outside Europe compared to hyperscale competitors

faqs.question: Where is Hetzner based?
faqs.answer: Hetzner Online is headquartered in Gunzenhausen, Germany, and was founded in 1997.

faqs.question: What server types does Hetzner offer?
faqs.answer: Dedicated servers (EX, AX, RX, SX, GPU, DX lines) and three tiers of cloud servers.

faqs.question: Are firewalls included?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hetzner includes free stateful firewalls with its cloud servers.

faqs.question: Does Hetzner support one-click app installs?
faqs.answer: Yes, including Docker, WordPress, Nextcloud, and GitLab.

use_cases.title: Low-cost dedicated hosting
use_cases.description: Rent dedicated hardware across several performance and price tiers.

use_cases.title: Test and business cloud workloads
use_cases.description: Choose Cost-Optimized, Regular, or General Purpose cloud servers based on workload needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hetzner?
paragraphs: Hetzner Online, founded in 1997 and headquartered in Gunzenhausen, Germany, is a hosting provider offering dedicated servers, cloud servers, storage, and web hosting.
It markets itself around affordable, high-performance infrastructure from Germany, with free firewalls, one-click app installs, and load balancing bundled into its cloud offerings.

enrichment.short_description: Scaleway is a European cloud and AI platform offering virtual instances, bare metal, GPU/AI compute, storage, Kubernetes, and managed databases.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Scaleway offers European cloud compute, bare metal, GPU/AI instances, storage, Kubernetes, and managed databases across four EU regions.

features.title: Virtual instances
features.description: General purpose, development, compute-optimized, and memory-optimized instance types.

features.title: Bare metal
features.description: Dedibox dedicated servers, Elastic Metal, and Apple Mac mini hosting options.

features.title: GPU/AI compute
features.description: GPU instances (including L40S, L4, H100, B300-SXM), GPU clusters, and generative AI APIs.

features.title: Managed databases
features.description: Managed PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, and MongoDB, plus serverless SQL.

features.title: Kubernetes
features.description: Kubernetes Kapsule and Kubernetes Kosmos, plus serverless containers.

features.title: Storage
features.description: Object storage, block storage, file storage, and Glacier cold storage.

pros: European data sovereignty with four multi-AZ EU cloud regions
Broad AI/GPU lineup including H100 and B300-SXM instances
Over 100 cloud and AI products/services on one platform
65+ global points of presence for networking/edge services
cons: The pricing overview page does not display specific prices, routing users to per-category pricing pages instead
Headquarters and company details are not on the homepage; found only via legal/careers pages

faqs.question: Where is Scaleway based?
faqs.answer: Scaleway's registered office is in Paris, France.

faqs.question: Does Scaleway offer GPU instances?
faqs.answer: Yes, including L40S, L4, H100, and B300-SXM GPU instances plus GPU clusters.

faqs.question: Does Scaleway show pricing on its pricing page?
faqs.answer: The pricing overview page links out to category-specific pricing pages rather than listing prices directly.

faqs.question: What databases does Scaleway manage?
faqs.answer: Managed PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, and MongoDB, along with serverless SQL.

use_cases.title: EU data-sovereign workloads
use_cases.description: Run infrastructure entirely within European cloud regions.

use_cases.title: AI/ML training and inference
use_cases.description: Use GPU instances and generative AI APIs for model training and inference.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Scaleway?
paragraphs: Scaleway is a European cloud and AI platform, headquartered in Paris, France, offering over 100 cloud and AI products including virtual instances, bare metal, GPU compute, storage, Kubernetes, and managed databases.
It emphasizes European data sovereignty, operating four multi-AZ cloud regions in Europe and citing customers such as Mistral AI and Hugging Face.

enrichment.short_description: UpCloud is a European cloud infrastructure provider offering cloud servers, managed Kubernetes, and databases with fixed pricing and zero-cost data transfer.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UpCloud offers European cloud servers from $3/month with zero-cost egress, a 14-day free trial, and 15 global data centers.

features.title: Cloud Servers
features.description: Starter, Premium, and Cloud Native server plans running on 5th Gen AMD EPYC 9575F processors.

features.title: Zero-cost data transfer
features.description: All plans include zero-cost egress bandwidth with no hidden fees.

features.title: MaxIOPS storage
features.description: Proprietary storage technology supporting up to 16 attached devices and 64TB total.

features.title: Snapshots and backups
features.description: Daily snapshots included, with paid 7/30/365-day retention options.

features.title: Global data centers
features.description: Infrastructure deployed across 15 global data centers.

features.title: Management tooling
features.description: Control panel, API, SDKs, CLI, and Terraform integration for infrastructure management.

pros: Zero-cost egress across all plans, unusual among cloud providers
14-day free trial with €250 in free credit and no commitment
Fixed, hourly-capped billing (max 672 hours/month) keeps costs predictable
Runs on recent-generation AMD EPYC hardware with DDR5 RAM
cons: Smaller global footprint (15 data centers) versus larger hyperscalers
Cloud Native plans exclude bundled storage/IP, requiring separate configuration for Kubernetes use

faqs.question: Does UpCloud have a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial with €250 in free credit and no commitment.

faqs.question: How much do UpCloud servers cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start from $3/month, billed hourly and capped at 672 hours per month.

faqs.question: Where is UpCloud headquartered?
faqs.answer: UpCloud was formed in 2011 and is headquartered in Helsinki, Finland.

faqs.question: Does UpCloud charge for data transfer?
faqs.answer: No, all Cloud Server plans include zero-cost egress.

use_cases.title: Cost-conscious dev/test hosting
use_cases.description: Use Starter plans for development, testing, and self-hosting workloads.

use_cases.title: Production-grade workloads
use_cases.description: Use Premium plans designed for business-critical performance and consistency.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is UpCloud?
paragraphs: UpCloud, formed in 2011 and headquartered in Helsinki, Finland, is a European cloud infrastructure provider offering cloud servers, managed Kubernetes, and databases.
It differentiates on fixed, hourly-capped pricing starting at $3/month, zero-cost data egress, and a 14-day free trial with €250 in credit.

enrichment.short_description: Kamatera is a cloud infrastructure provider offering scalable virtual machines, private cloud, load balancers, and managed cloud services.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kamatera offers enterprise cloud servers from $4/month, a 30-day free trial with up to $100 credit, and hourly or monthly billing.

features.title: Cloud servers
features.description: Basic, Standard, and Pro server tiers with NVMe SSD storage and 5TB traffic included.

features.title: Virtual Private Cloud
features.description: Isolated private cloud environments for tenants needing network separation.

features.title: Cloud Firewall
features.description: Security protection layered onto cloud servers.

features.title: Load Balancers
features.description: Distribute traffic across multiple servers.

features.title: Block Storage
features.description: Centralized, attachable data storage for servers.

features.title: Managed Cloud Services
features.description: Kamatera's team manages infrastructure on behalf of customers.

pros: 30-day free trial with up to $100 in credit and no long-term contract required
Entry-level server priced at $4/month
Both hourly (billed by the minute) and monthly billing options
Managed services available for teams that don't want to self-manage infrastructure
cons: Headquarters location isn't clearly stated on the site, only regional sales/support phone numbers
Entry Basic and Standard/Pro tiers all include the same 20GB storage, so extra storage is billed separately at $0.05/GB/month

faqs.question: Does Kamatera offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 30-day free trial with up to $100 in credit on one server.

faqs.question: How much does Kamatera cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $4/month for a Basic 1 vCPU / 1GB RAM server.

faqs.question: Is there a long-term contract?
faqs.answer: No, Kamatera states no long-term contracts are required.

faqs.question: Does Kamatera offer managed services?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kamatera's Managed Cloud Services let its team manage customer infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Scalable VPS hosting
use_cases.description: Start with a low-cost server and scale resources as workloads grow.

use_cases.title: Isolated private cloud
use_cases.description: Deploy a Virtual Private Cloud environment for network-isolated workloads.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Kamatera?
paragraphs: Kamatera, launched in 1996, is a cloud infrastructure provider offering virtual machines, Virtual Private Cloud, load balancers, block storage, and managed cloud services across multiple global data centers.
Plans start at $4/month, with both monthly and per-minute hourly billing, and a 30-day free trial that includes up to $100 in server credit.

enrichment.short_description: Clever Cloud is a French sovereign PaaS for deploying and operating applications and managed databases with per-second, pay-as-you-go billing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clever Cloud is a French PaaS for deploying apps and managed databases, billed by the second, with free trial credits and no card required.

features.title: Application runtimes
features.description: Supports 15+ languages including Node.js, Python, Java, Go, Ruby, Rust, PHP, .NET Core, and Docker.

features.title: Managed databases
features.description: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, and Cassandra, plus proprietary Materia products.

features.title: Object storage
features.description: Cellar Object Storage, File System Buckets, and Pulsar messaging.

features.title: One-click deployment
features.description: Automated deployment from Git with automatic language detection and environment configuration.

features.title: Clever AI
features.description: A platform aggregating LLMs with prompt orchestration.

features.title: Compliance
features.description: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified, HDS (Health Data Hosting) and GDPR compliant, with an optional SecNumCloud zone.

pros: Per-second billing tied to actual usage rather than fixed tiers
Free trial credits at signup with no payment card required
Broad compliance certifications (ISO 27001, HDS, GDPR, SecNumCloud option)
99.90% SLA, rising to 99.99% on the Premium tier
cons: No fixed pricing plans or numeric rates are published; customers must use a cost calculator or contact sales
Sovereign/compliance focus is centered on the EU, which may be less relevant for non-European customers

faqs.question: How is Clever Cloud billed?
faqs.answer: By the second, reflecting actual usage, with users setting a maximum spend cap.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, signing up grants free credits with no payment card required.

faqs.question: Where is Clever Cloud based?
faqs.answer: Clever Cloud was founded in 2010 and is headquartered in Nantes, France.

faqs.question: What databases are supported?
faqs.answer: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, and Cassandra, among others.

use_cases.title: Deploying apps without managing servers
use_cases.description: Push code via Git and let Clever Cloud handle builds, scaling, and updates.

use_cases.title: Compliance-sensitive hosting
use_cases.description: Run workloads under ISO 27001, HDS, and GDPR-compliant French infrastructure.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Clever Cloud?
paragraphs: Clever Cloud, founded in 2010 in Nantes, France, is a sovereign, fully managed Platform-as-a-Service for deploying and operating applications and managed databases.
It bills per second based on actual usage, supports 15+ language runtimes, and holds ISO 27001, HDS, and GDPR compliance certifications for regulated workloads.

enrichment.short_description: Porter is a Platform-as-a-Service that lets teams deploy and scale applications on their own AWS, GCP, or Azure account with Git-based CI/CD.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Porter is a PaaS for deploying apps to your own AWS, GCP, or Azure account, with Git-based CI/CD, preview environments, and usage-based pricing.

features.title: Git/Docker deployment
features.description: Deploy applications directly from Git or Docker with automatic builds and CI/CD.

features.title: Preview environments
features.description: Isolated preview environments generated for every pull request.

features.title: Zero-downtime deploys
features.description: Zero-downtime deployments with automatic Kubernetes cluster upgrades.

features.title: Autoscaling
features.description: Automatic scaling of applications based on load.

features.title: Jobs and cron jobs
features.description: Support for scheduled and one-off job execution.

features.title: Compliance
features.description: SOC 2 Type II certified, with one-click HIPAA compliance options.

pros: Runs inside the customer's own AWS, GCP, or Azure account rather than a shared platform
Usage-based pricing means no charge for unused resources
Automatic preview environments per pull request
SOC 2 Type II certified with a one-click HIPAA option
cons: Porter's fees are separate from and additional to the underlying cloud provider's own infrastructure costs
Enterprise plan pricing is custom/quote-only, requiring contact with sales
No general free tier; only a startup program offering 6 months free for eligible companies

faqs.question: Where does Porter run applications?
faqs.answer: Inside the customer's own AWS, GCP, or Azure cloud account, not a shared Porter-hosted environment.

faqs.question: How is Porter priced?
faqs.answer: Usage-based: $6/month per GB of RAM and $13/month per vCPU, on the Standard plan, plus underlying cloud provider costs.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Porter's startup program offers 6 months free rather than a standard free trial.

faqs.question: Does Porter support HIPAA compliance?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers a one-click HIPAA compliance option alongside SOC 2 Type II certification.

use_cases.title: Deploying to your own cloud account
use_cases.description: Run production apps on your own AWS/GCP/Azure account without managing Kubernetes directly.

use_cases.title: PR-based preview environments
use_cases.description: Automatically spin up isolated environments for every pull request.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Porter?
paragraphs: Porter is a Platform-as-a-Service that runs applications inside a customer's own AWS, GCP, or Azure account, handling networking, CI/CD, and autoscaling on top of Kubernetes.
It bills on a usage basis ($6/month per GB RAM, $13/month per vCPU) separate from the underlying cloud provider's costs, and is SOC 2 Type II certified with an Enterprise tier for larger deployments.
